# assertForOverFulfill

**Instance Property**

Indicates that an assertion should be triggered during testing if the expectation is over-fulfilled.

## Declaration

```swift
var assertForOverFulfill: Bool { get set }
```

## Discussion

When [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true], a call to [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation/fulfill()] made after the expectation has already been fulfilled (exceeding [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation/expectedFulfillmentCount]) will trigger an assertion. When [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false], a call to [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation/fulfill()] after the expectation has already been fulfilled will have no effect.

The [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation/assertForOverFulfill] property is set to [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false] by default for expectations created directly from initializers on [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation] and its subclasses.

The [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation/assertForOverFulfill] property is set to [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] by default for expectations created with the following [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase] convenience methods:

- [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase/expectation(description:)]
- [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase/expectation(for:evaluatedWith:handler:)]
- [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase/expectation(forNotification:object:handler:)]
- [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase/keyValueObservingExpectation(for:keyPath:expectedValue:)]
- [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase/keyValueObservingExpectation(for:keyPath:handler:)]

## Fulfillment Count

- **expectedFulfillmentCount**: The number of times [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestExpectation/fulfill()] must be called before the expectation is completely fulfilled.
